Hey team, in this video we're going to start prepping for the lead machine. We're going to show you what accounts you need to get set up and how to do some basic tasks to get ready for it. So the first account we're going to walk you through is creating your ClickFunnels account. That's going to be where you do a lot of your ad funnels, membership funnels for fulfillment. We're going to show you how to set up your acuity scheduling account, which is where your clients will be able to schedule directly onto the calendar and trigger some awesome automations later on. We're going to show you how to create Zapier, which is like a glue program that makes one piece of software, talk to another piece of software, trigger based on somebody signing up or an appointment coming up or created. There's a lot of really cool things. We're just going to create the account though right now. Skipyo, we're going to walk you through how to create your Skipyo account, which is great for sending SMS messages, MMS messages. We also show you how to do a really cool setup with some of the automation campaigns later on. Skipyo is US only, which can be a little frustrating. We do apologize for that, but hopefully it should provide some great information. We're going to show you how to get Skipyo talking to Zapier. There's a little setup process you have to do for that. We're going to walk you through creating a Facebook page if you don't already have one. I'm going to show you how to block comments automatically on that Facebook page. We're going to show you how to create a Facebook business manager account as well, and then add a payment to that account, specifically the ad account, so you can start running ads as soon as you're ready. Then we're going to show you how to upload some unlisted YouTube videos, which you'll need to do for any of your challenges that are coming up, or if you're sending any messages. A lot of times those ad funnels are going to have YouTube videos, and you'll want to make sure you have those ready ahead of time. Hey team, we're going to get your ClickFunnels account set up now. Look below this video for a button that takes you right to the ClickFunnels sign up. It should take you to a page that looks similar to this. They may change the design at some point, but you're looking for a button that says start your free 14-day trial. So if you use that link below, you get a free 14-day trial. It gives us credit for it too, so please use that link. Once you click on the button to go and start your trial, I'm going to ask you for some basic information. Your full name, so go and enter in your full name, your email address, your email address, and then a good password. Then click the get started button. It's going to go to have you entering your billing information now. That's going to be the phone number for your billing information, your card that you're using, address, and then the card number. Now they don't bill you right away, but it will automatically bill you once that 14-day trial is completed. You do not have to add the nine secret funnels training. So let's go and call this. Best appointment. Now duration is however long you want that appointment to be. Blocking off extra time before or after. That means it's going to automatically schedule in extra time before or after. So say for instance you're doing these new orientations and you want at least 15 minutes before they actually show up. Where you're not with somebody else. You can do this block off extra time and then before. But in 15 minutes. Or you can do the same for after. It's whatever you want to set it up as. And if you don't want anything there, just change that back to zero. Normally for price, you don't want to put a price there because if you do put a price, it's going to show them the price when they go to schedule. That can scare away some people. It's normally better to just leave prices blank. Now you can choose a picture for this specific appointment type. So if you're doing this for a challenge or something in particular and you have a picture, you want your clients to see when they schedule it. You can choose file and pick a picture from your computer. Access, you almost always want that to be public. Public means that anybody is able to access that and schedule it with a link. And here you can choose whether or not you want it to show the daily appearance, which is probably the best options. Unless you're having them book way far in advance and then monthly may be a better option. Normally you want to do daily business. You can put in whatever your business name is. For logo, you can choose a logo for your gym. For scheduling page options, you normally want to uncheck show schedule another appointment button because you only want one appointment per client normally. You do want to disable recurring appointments so you don't want clients to pick more than one time by accident. You want to disable client login and registration. That means they're not going to be prompted to create an account which can cause confusion. And they may ask you, hey, I need to reset this password or they may think it's for a different service. So normally it's better to disable that client login and registration. Now appointment type list, hide duration. You can hide that if you want. That just means that where it would show the duration of it, you can hide it. And that way it doesn't show it right away. And then collapse categories, you normally don't want to do that. Let's go ahead and move on to the next step. Now I'm going to show you how to set up your Zapier account and some of the basic features. I'm not going to go too in depth in it because Zapier has a lot of depth to it. It allows you to connect just about any software platform to any other software platform. It does take a little bit of work and knowledge and know-how. A lot of times when we have you Zapier, we're going to provide pre-made templates. There you have a lot of the steps filled out. So that way it's a bit easier on you. Go ahead and head to zapier.com. There's no referral link for this. We'll give you a button below as well to take you right to Zapier. When you get to Zapier, look at the top right and you're looking for sign up. Now if you have a Google account, you can choose to sign up with Google. That's going to make it a lot quicker. I'm going to go and do that here. Otherwise you can do sign up and just sign up with a normal email address and password like you wouldn't. So I'm going to hit the sign up with Google because they provide normally more than one, more than those two steps. You're going to need three or four steps. So you just use upgrade plan. It's going to walk you through setting up the payment. This is the annual. You can also do pay monthly. Once you're inside of Zapier, you can do dashboard, which is going to show you just kind of the let's get started with Zapier. You can go to my zaps, which is going to list all of the zaps that you have running. Those are the sets of actions. You can create folders. So if you want to organize your zaps, you can. Otherwise it's just all going to go into this main home folder. Task history is going to show you all of the tasks. So if a zap runs, it's going to show you what that zap did. And you can click on it and get more information. That's a great way to troubleshoot if you're not sure if your zaps are working. My apps is going to show you all of the different accounts you've connected with Zapier. What I do recommend is if you're signed in with a Google account that you're going to use for everything, you have all of your files and everything like that. You want to choose connect all my Google apps. That's going to make it a lot easier when you set up your Zapier account and start making those apps because they're already going to be connected there. You won't have to go through all those steps. So I chose my right account. It's going to ask for a lot of permissions because it's connecting all of your Google services and then hit allow. You see it automatically adds in all of those account connections. So that way it's a lot easier in the future when you go to either set up a zap from one of the templates that we make or you're making one on your own. You can. You can also set up to automatically email you or put them in one campaign or another. So Zapier is super powerful, super robust. And we've actually already created a starter list that you can use to block some bad words that you may want to avoid showing up in any comments on your page. So open up Facebook and open up your page if you're not there already. So I'm at Facebook. I'm going to hit Pages on the left. I found my page and I'm going to click on it. And then at the top right, we want to click on Settings right there. And under Settings, we want to look for Page Moderation. It says No words are blocked or being blocked from the page. I'm going to click Edit on that. And that gives us a place to add in words or phrases to block. Below this video, we're going to give you a button that says Facebook Negative Comments Blocklist. Once you click on that, it's going to take you to this page. Go ahead and make a copy. This is going to make a copy of it to your Google Drive. That way you own this and you can make that change. So what I'm going to do is highlight this entire list and go to Copy it to my keyboard and to my clipboard. That's Control C on a Windows computer. Command C on a Mac. I'm going to head back to my page settings and where it asks us to put in the words. I'm going to paste it and then click this Add button over here on the right. If you're in a place where you can add all of your members to, you're often going to send this link to them. That way they're able to collect in one spot. You can share information for everybody. You can even break into different groups. That way you have everybody who's starting on the same day in the same group. So it's okay to have more than one group and just change out the link as you go. I'm going to walk you through creating one group. You can name it whatever you like. I'm going to use just an example placeholder name, but make sure you choose something that's really relevant and specific to your offer. How's it going everyone? In this video, we're going to cover creating the Facebook group that you're going to use for this play. This Facebook group is going to be a great central place for everybody participating to be able to share their successes, support each other, and just get everybody hyped up. It's also a great place to keep track of how to get in touch with the people participating over Facebook as well. If you already are familiar with how to create a Facebook group, how to change the announcement, the cover photo, invite people to it, approve posts and stories, you can probably skip this. Otherwise follow along and we'll walk you through step by step. Go ahead and get logged into the Facebook account that you want to use as the admin for this group, the main account. On this left hand side, you want to find groups. Now if you have a bunch of groups or apps installed, you may have to scroll down a bit to see it, but under explore, you should see groups as a little icon with the people on it. Go ahead and click on that. It's going to take you to groups. If you have a whole bunch of groups that you're a part of, you're going to see them listed here. If you don't, that's okay. At the top right, you want to look for the screen create group button. Once you click on that green create group at the top right, let's go to open this box where you can name your group. We're going to choose a group name that's very specific to this, so that way you know exactly which one it is. Now whenever you create a group, you do have to invite at least one person to it. So this would be a great time to invite one of your managers or trainers. It's going to be a part of this challenge. That way you have somebody else who can help you monitor and manage it as well. So let's go ahead and add in somebody. And again, at least one person has to be there. And right now it's set to close group. Anyone can find the group and see who runs it, but only members can see who's in it and what they post. We're going to go ahead and click on that and go ahead and change it to secret group. Only members can find the group, see who's in it, and what they post. That way you can keep things restricted. Either one is fine. It's just up to you whether or not you want people to be able to actually see this group. If this looks good, go ahead and hit the blue create button. Now one thing to note is this pin to shortcuts over here. If you put a check mark there, that means it's going to put it at the top of the list once you log into Facebook. That way it's easy to find this group. And while this challenge is active, I highly recommend you do that. That way it's always in the front of your mind when you look at Facebook and you remember to check it and make sure you're taking care of it. Everything looks good here. Let's go ahead and hit this blue create button. Now take a couple moments and then take you to your new group. You can see the name over there and you can start making changes. One of the first things you want to do to personalize your group is upload a photo to it. So I'm going to hit this upload photo. And I'm going to go and put in a logo. Now you can drag it around to reposition if you need to and then hit save. Of course yours is going to look a lot better than that, I hope. We also want to do a welcome announcement. This will be the first post that's always at the top and it tells people what's going on. To make an announcement, you make a post and then you set that post as the announcement. So on the main page of your group, the discussion tab, where it says write something, that's where you'll put your post at. You want to make sure to include a good positive message. You want to include the challenge dates, when the challenge starts, ends, the way out, all of the relevant information that they need to know. Once you get that post in there, I'm just going to put in something quick. Make sure yours is pretty informational. You can use lots of emojis to hype them up. Once you have that post, look for it. And then right there is a drop down arrow. Click on that arrow and choose mark as announcement. So that's going to make that post the first one that everybody sees when they get to the group. So that's all it is to make an announcement. So now that announcement post, even if other people post that announcements always go to be at the top. The next one we're going to look at is members. If you look on this menu on the left, you have members. Go ahead and click on that. Now members is where you can see anybody who's a member of your group. You can see the post that they make with this view recent posts link right there. And you can also remove them from the group or make them a moderator. So say you have a trainer that's helping you out with this, or you have somebody who's left the challenge, you can change it there. So you just want to look for the person that you need to make changes to. And to the right of their name will be the three dots. When you click on that, it gives you the option to change it to moderator or leave group. Now, since I'm the admin and this is my account, that's why you see that remove as admin. Normally, you'd never want to remove as admin because that means you lose the overall control of the group. So always focus on this change to moderator or leave group and make sure you're making only people you really trust the moderators because they'll be able to control the posts and who joins it. So it's best to only give that to people who are actually employed by your gym and participating as part of this challenge. Or if you have somebody who handles your social media, maybe you want to make them a moderator. Here under all members, you can remove them from the group too. You don't see that since we don't have other people here, but that's going to be an option there once you get other people joining too. So that's how you get rid of somebody if you need to. On this left hand side is moderate group. I'm not going to go and click on that. This gives you a chance to go through all of the different options with it. So we have admin activity, which shows anything you've recently done. You have scheduled posts. So if you want to make it to where automatically post something later on, you can add a scheduled post in here or at least see and manage them. Whenever somebody requests to join the group, because you're going to be providing the link to this group to people, whenever they request it, you'll see it under member requests, and you'll be able to approve or deny those. Approval notifications will set the settings for letting you know if somebody gets notified or requests to do it. I always recommend to leave this as get notifications, that way you can respond as quick as possible. You can set up rules. Now we did put some of the rules inside of the announcement post, but if you want to put your rules here, you can set them up under this create rules as well. Right here where it says member reported, whenever somebody reports a post or a story or a comment, it's going to show up here. That way you can take a look at it, see what's going on. And here we have auto flagged. That means that Facebook automatically detected that whatever this post or comment or story was did not meet their standards. It's going to pop here automatically. Hopefully you'll never have to deal with that. So I'm going to go back to the group by clicking the group name right there. I'm going to click this more right here. And so here we can add members. So if you want to add somebody directly, you can go to moderate group, which is that same as right there. But we're going to go to edit group settings. So let's go ahead and hit edit group settings. If you want to give a type of your group, go ahead and do that. Since this is a private group, you don't have to worry about that. Group type is really just for helping people find your group. The same is for description. It lets people know what your group is about if you want them to be able to find them. For location, I do recommend adding a location and choosing your gym in here. That way they know where to meet up in case they ever forget. If you want to change your group between secret or public or private, you can click that change privacy, make the change and hit confirm. We're going to leave it as secret for now, but you can change that to closed if you need to or you can just click on it. You can change that to closed if you need to or if you chose the wrong one before, that's how you can fix it. Membership approval. That means anybody in the group is able to approve people joining the group. Normally you don't want that. So I'm going to change this to only admins and moderators are able to approve new members. Get a chance to review it before allowing it to go to everybody in the group. So the only real options we changed in here, we put a check mark next to post approval and a check mark next to story post approval. This looks great. So I'm going to go ahead and hit this blue save button and it lets you know that your changes have been saved. Now I want to show you how to get the link for this group. That way you have it to share later on. If you click right here on your group title at the top left, it'll take you back to the main page. And up here at the top left in the address bar, that URL, that link, that's the one that you use to go directly to this group. You want to select it all so you can click and drag with your mouse and copy it to your clipboard. If you're using a tracking document, you can take that link and put that right in the tracking document. That way you have it all in one place. Otherwise you can go ahead and share it with other people, either email. And you just want to put in whatever yours is. Let's go ahead and hit that next. Let's go and ask you to go and enter in your location information. So for country, go ahead and choose your country. I'm going to choose United States. And go ahead and put in your address. It is very important you put in the right address for your business. That way you can use it. It gives you a lot of issues if you don't use the right address. It also uses that for taxing taxes and billing purposes. So make sure you're using the right business address. You can also enter in a business phone number. And then you can also put in your business website if you want to. So as far as business use, you're probably going to be promoting your own goods or services. Once everything's good here, you double check your business details. Go ahead and hit this blue submit button at the bottom right. Looks like we need to put it in the website. There we go. So it does want you to confirm your email address. So you'll want to head to your email account and confirm that really quick. And then once you have it confirmed, you can hit that done. It should take you right here to your business manager account. So we want to go ahead and add a page right here where it says set up your business manager. Let's go and add a page. We're going to choose add a page that you want and hit this add page button. And also choose to create a page here as well. We've already done that hopefully so you can just add your existing one. I want to know the Facebook page name so you can go to Facebook. And you can go to pages over here on the left. Let's go into list all of your pages. Go ahead and click on your page. Then up here at the top is the address of this page. I'm going to select that all, copy it to my clipboard. It's control C on a Windows computer or command C on a Mac. I'm going to paste it right here where it says URL. Now it's going to try to suggest the right business and it did suggest the right one. So I'm going to go and click on that and then click this add page button. Since we're already an administrator on that page since we already own it, it automatically connects it. If that page is owned by somebody else, you have to jump through some hoops or requests to access. The person who owns that page had for the business we've connected already or for a different business. That's going to be for the business we've connected already. So let's go ahead and hit this create once we select that. Now you want to give it permissions. So you want to give it permission to manage campaigns and manage ad accounts. You want to select your name over here on the left. So that way you're giving yourself permission to edit that ad account through business manager. So make sure all of those are ticked to the right. They're highlighted. Make sure you select your name on the right and then go and hit this assign button right there. It gives you your ad account number and then adds it to the business manager account. So now with your business manager account, you're able to manage your page and your Facebook ad account all together. Before you're ever able to run any ads or even really create ads on your account, you need to add a payment method to your ad account. So now that your ad account is linked to your business manager account, either because you created a new one or you added it specifically, go into Facebook business manager. That's business.facebook.com. Once you get here, look at the top right for this blue business settings button. Once you click on that, it's probably going to open a new tab on the left hand side. We have this accounts. If it's not expanded, go and click on to expand it. And you want to click on ad accounts right there. And here's our Facebook ad account. Over here at the top right, it says open an ads manager on a little arrow. I'm going to go and click that drop down arrow and click view payment methods. And then here under payment methods, you want to click this add payment method button. Go and connect either a credit or debit card, PayPal, direct debit from a bank or a Facebook ad coupon. That way you're able to go ahead and add in that billing information and you can start creating your Facebook ads right away. If you have other payment methods already listed here, you're able to change the order that they're charged. Or delete one or more. But make sure you always have at least one good active payment method. Otherwise your ads aren't going to run. To share a video, you need a link to that video. And so the best way to share a video that's either personalized for a client, very specific to a challenge, or you're putting inside an advertising or membership funnel is to upload it as an unlisted YouTube video. Unlisted means that it's going to show up on your page when you view it. But when other people view your page, they won't see it. And if you give somebody the link to that video, they're able to view that video. So go ahead to youtube.com, login if you need to. Once you're on YouTube, go and look for this camera icon that has a plus on it. When you move your mouse over, it says create a video or post. Once you click on that. In the menu that opens up, go and click on upload video. It's going to take you to the upload screen. And right here you can change the privacy settings. By default it's going to be set to public. Go and click on that and change it to unlisted. Once we have that set, I'm going to click this upload arrow. To choose my video, it's going to start uploading it right away. You can change the name of the video. You can give a description if you want. If you didn't choose unlisted, you can do it over here on the right hand side. You can change that to unlisted. Under advanced settings, you can uncheck allow comments, uncheck view ratings, uncheck publish the subscriptions, and uncheck make video statistics on the watch page visible. I always recommend unchecking those options. That way the information isn't displayed to your users if they go directly to the link. It's just something they normally don't need to see. Your video may take some time to upload and process. That's okay. Mine was a really short video, so it didn't take long at all. If you look right here, it has the link to the video, so you can select that and copy it, put it right where you need to, or put it on your tracking document if you're using your tracking document. You can also hit done, and it'll give you that same link right there. You can select it again, copy it, and then share it from there. That's how you upload an unlisted YouTube video. That way you're able to send it to a client or put it in an ad funnel, but you don't have to have it showing up on your page and cluttering it up when somebody looks at your YouTube channel.
